summary:
The lingual ridge tends to develop prominently on canines due to functional demands; however, there’s a notable similarity in prominence when comparing upper versus lower canines once fully erupted at approximately pubertal age.
The prominence level of lingual ridges appears equally distributed between upper and lower canines shortly after tooth eruption, emphasizing their role rather than positional advantage or disadvantage. Based solely on this information about dental anatomy and physiological maturity timelines, it cannot definitively state one area has inherently greater prominence without additional context-specific
